numeric_violations.claim: iPad users in educational settings (students, teachers) represent a significant affected segment, with one user estimating 60-70% of students have writable devices
Users report losing critical time-sensitive data including exam materials due within 2 days, university lecture notes 1 month before finals, and multi-year work spanning 3+ years
Users report losing critical time-sensitive data including exam materials due within 2 days, university lecture notes 1 month before finals, and multi-year work spanning 3+ years
18 users rated the app 1-2 stars specifically citing iPad keyboard issues
21 reports originated from Canada, representing 43% of the 49 total items in this theme
Immediately investigate authentication flow for data loss bug, prioritizing Canadian and British user sessions from 2021-2026 timeframe
Conduct accessibility audit of mobile app against WCAG 2.1 AA text scaling requirements

faithfulness_details.claim: Users report that AI features now dominate the homepage and primary interface, replacing customizable layouts they previously created
Users cannot disable or opt-out of AI feature visibility, with persistent 'Ask AI' prompts appearing throughout the interface
Multiple users report degraded search performance and increased app slowness attributed to AI integration
Long-time users are actively migrating to competing platforms specifically due to forced AI features
Users perceive aggressive upselling of paid AI features as spam that interferes with their workflow
Users consistently praise Notion's versatility for organizing multiple life domains (work, school, personal projects, budgeting, recipes)
Multiple users report improved academic performance and productivity after adopting Notion
Learning curve exists but users find value once they master the app's features
Users report the app crashes immediately on launch or fails to open, particularly after updates
Users are stuck in an uninterruptible onboarding tutorial that prevents app access
Recent updates introduce new crashes and workflow-breaking bugs on mobile
App crashes during active use, causing work loss and productivity disruption
Users cannot access app support because support links open the crashing app
Apple Pencil handwriting input is broken or non-functional for users attempting to write text, with reports of text not appearing, missing spaces between words, or writing not registering at all
Users expect native inking/drawing features to annotate diagrams and papers, explicitly comparing the app unfavorably to GoodNotes and Notability which offer this functionality
Apple Pencil interaction behavior is erratic, including unintended typing on wrong lines, scrolling triggering text input, and UI controls requiring multiple taps to activate
Apple Pencil Scribble feature (system-level handwriting recognition) is reported as broken
iPad users in educational settings (students, teachers) represent a significant affected segment, with one user estimating 60-70% of students have writable devices
Users cannot complete editing workflows without force-closing the iOS app, with freezing occurring during template editing, page building, and typing
unsupported_details: The claim states users 'cannot complete editing workflows' - the sources describe disruptions and frustrations but do not explicitly state complete inability to finish workflows
The claim specifies 'force-closing' as necessary - sources mention 'manually close' (Review 1) and 'close out and reopen' (Review 3) but don't use the specific term 'force-closing'
The claim specifies 'typing' as a context where freezing occurs - no source explicitly mentions freezing during typing specifically

faithfulness_details.claim: iPad app exhibits severe lag and unresponsiveness, with users reporting deleted work and inability to select items correctly
Pages refresh repeatedly during modification, preventing users from scrolling or making changes to databases and lists
Performance issues affect both free and paid subscription users across iPhone and iPad devices
Users report progressively worsening performance over time, with scrolling lockups occurring even on simple pages
Search functionality triggers crashes on mobile
Performance issues span multiple iOS versions and device generations, including iPhone 13 mini, 8th gen iPad, iPhone 17 Pro Max on iOS 26.3
Users experience unexpected logout events that trigger complete loss of all pages, notes, and workspaces upon re-authentication
Lost data is not present in trash functionality and cannot be recovered by support team
unsupported_details: Cannot be recovered by support team - not explicitly stated in source reviews

faithfulness_details.claim: App updates appear to correlate with unexpected logout and subsequent data loss events
unsupported_details: The claim uses plural 'events' suggesting multiple documented occurrences, but only one review is provided
The claim states updates 'appear to correlate' which implies a pattern across multiple cases, but the source only describes one user's experience
The claim specifies 'unexpected logout' but the review mentions being 'asked to make an account' which suggests a deliberate prompt rather than an unexpected logout

faithfulness_details.claim: Users report losing critical time-sensitive data including exam materials due within 2 days, university lecture notes 1 month before finals, and multi-year work spanning 3+ years

The claim states '11 reports' from Great Britain but no geographic location information appears in any source review

faithfulness_details.claim: Users report being prompted to pay for premium to access version history after data loss
Users report the mobile app directly ports desktop UI without mobile optimization, causing usability problems
New users struggle with onboarding and navigation, requiring external resources like YouTube to learn basic functionality
Users cannot perform basic mobile operations like copy/paste and name changes, forcing them to use desktop
Users actively prefer or exclusively use the desktop version due to mobile app limitations
Users report abandoning the product entirely due to mobile-only usage constraints
unsupported_details: No reviews explicitly state they abandoned the product due to 'mobile-only usage constraints'
Review 1 mentions deleting due to difficulty with copy/paste functionality, not mobile-only constraints
Review 2 mentions downloading other apps due to complexity, not mobile-only usage limitations
Review 3 mentions desktop might be easier and criticizes the phone app experience, but does not report abandoning the product entirely
The claim specifically asserts 'mobile-only usage constraints' as the reason for abandonment, but no review describes being constrained to mobile-only usage

faithfulness_details.claim: Users cannot see what they are typing because the viewport auto-scrolls or jumps to hide the active input area
The cursor appears in the wrong position or does not appear at all when users tap to place it
Text input becomes completely disabled, requiring users to force-close and restart the app to regain typing functionality
Scrolling triggers unwanted viewport jumps that reposition users to unintended page locations
External keyboard input does not work correctly on mobile, forcing users to use on-screen keyboard
Switching input methods (stylus to finger) triggers the bug that prevents further text input
Users report payment processed but subscription not recognized, blocking access to paid features
Mobile app subscription does not transfer to web application, requiring users to cancel and resubscribe
Users cannot access billing or cancellation functionality from mobile app
Users report continued billing after cancellation
Support requires screen recording proof of cancellation to process refund disputes
Support is unresponsive or uses AI chat that does not resolve billing issues
unsupported_details: The claim specifically mentions 'billing issues' but the source reviews describe broader support problems including access issues, subscription problems, and service delivery failures, not exclusively billing disputes
The claim states support 'uses AI chat' but only Review 1 mentions 'AI chat' and does not specify this is the exclusive or primary support method
The claim implies AI chat is used broadly for support, but only one of three reviews mentions AI chat at all

faithfulness_details.claim: Cancellation flow captures additional payment during retention attempt
unsupported_details: The claim states payment was captured 'during retention attempt' but the source indicates payment was taken 'through a flow to try and stop me cancelling' - while similar, the source doesn't explicitly confirm this was a retention attempt specifically
The claim uses the term 'additional payment' but the source doesn't specify if this was an additional/extra payment versus a regular payment processed during the cancellation flow
